text: Hi, everyone. This is a short video to explain how you can create documents on Trupierre. What you'll need to do is basically... There are two modalities. One option is to start recording if you're making a new video, or if you're uploading a video, you can click on the upload option. Let's check out the upload a video flow. I click on Upload a video. It's a good practice to click after mentioning the step to ensure syncing and screenshotting happen properly. Now, I'll click on the Select a video option. This will open the file picker. Let me select a file from here and press Enter. I'll choose only the Generate Document option, not the Generate a Document and Video, just the Generate Document. Uncheck the Generate Video option. You'll see it only says Doc. That's what you'll need to use. Then I'll click on Submit. If you need to trim certain parts, a good approach for longer videos is to handle the introduction section. You can drag the pointer to where you want the actual document to start. Split it, delete the unnecessary part, save the trim, and move forward. Once you've done everything, click on the Submit option. This will upload the video. Now it's creating the document. Depending on how long your input video is, the trial account supports up to 60 minutes of video. It will generate the final document in roughly 10 to 15 minutes for an hour-long video. If it's a shorter video, the generation will be faster. Now, this has already generated the document. For example, I've set up this template and it auto-applied this template. If I need to modify something in this document, I can quickly go in. If I want to crop the video to a certain section of the screen, I click on the Crop Image option, which opens this window. I can select where I want to crop, say like this. To apply it to all images, click on Apply Crop to All Images and then Save. This will apply the crop to all images. If I want to highlight something in my documents, I can go to the Edit option and quickly add highlights. It's that easy. You can see the changes. Or if I want a different screenshot from the video, I can click on Add Image, move to a different part of the video, and click Add Screenshot. Editing the document is as simple as editing any Word document. You can type directly, highlight things, delete as needed, or add a screenshot by clicking on Add Image. To upload an external image, click on Upload Image and add something externally. All of this is much like working with a doc file. The easiest way is to make all edits here and directly download the final saved file. This makes your editing much faster and easier than editing in the downloaded Word document. That's how we work with uploading a video. Now, let me show you how to record a video. Click on the Start Recording button, which will open a pop-up. You can click Start Recording and choose whether to record a tab or the entire screen. Then click Share and the recording will start. After that, it's much the same as the upload option, where you choose whether to generate a document, video, or both. Make sure to uncheck video generation so it doesn't consume your AI minutes, and you can follow the same steps after that. Thank you.
